channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Uttar Pradesh mandates expiry and laying dates on eggs from April 1 to ensure public safety
item.description: The Uttar Pradesh government has issued a new order mandating that all eggs sold in the state must carry both the date on which they were laid and the expiry date, effective from April 1, 2026. Eggs t...

item.title: Technological Features Make Iranian Shahed Drones Difficult to Counter
item.description: Iranian-designed Shahed drones have emerged as a significant challenge in Middle Eastern conflicts due to their low cost, stealth features, and advanced navigation capabilities. Designed to explode on...

item.title: Calgary allocates $33 million to expand affordable housing for Indigenous residents
item.description: The City of Calgary has announced a $33‑million investment to support an Indigenous Housing Program aimed at increasing access to affordable housing for First Nations, Métis, and Inuit residents. City...

item.title: Picsart launches AI agent marketplace to help creators automate content and e-commerce tasks
item.description: Picsart, the AI-powered design platform with over 130 million users globally, has launched an AI agent marketplace to assist creators in automating various content and e-commerce tasks. This marketpla...

item.title: Tanzania Prepares Amateur Boxers for 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow
item.description: The Boxing Federation of Tanzania (BFT) is confident that Tanzanian amateur boxers are fully prepared to take part in the 2026 Commonwealth Games, set to happen from July 23 to August 2 in Glasgow, Sc...

item.title: LTFRB announces fare increases for public transportation starting March 19, 2026
item.description: Starting March 19, 2026, Filipinos will experience higher fares for almost all forms of public transportation due to rising fuel prices caused by the Middle East conflict. The Land Transportation Fran...

item.title: Owoseni Urges Strong Rule of Law and Good Governance for State Police Success
item.description: Fatai Owoseni, former Lagos Commissioner of Police, don talk say to make any state police work well for Nigeria, dem gats strengthen rule of law, dey follow good governance, plus proper funding. E yan...

item.title: Empowering Africa's Youth to Shape AI Innovation and Development
item.description: The article stresses that Africa's youth should not just consume AI tools but actively build them to secure the continent's future. It points out how AI is transforming industries worldwide, yet Afric...

item.title: Survivor recounts online child abuse amid rising cases of sexual exploitation
item.description: Nellie, now 24, shared her experience of being a victim of online child abuse at the age of 14. She sent three nude images over Snapchat during a relationship, believing it to be a normal practice amo...

item.title: Marites Vitug Raises Key Questions on President Marcos' Role in the Flood Control Corruption Scandal
item.description: With only two years remaining in his term, President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. is facing what many consider the biggest corruption scandal in Philippine history, centred on massive irregularitie...

item.title: How Grandparents Can Legally Boost Their Age Pension While Helping Grandkids Save for a First Home
item.description: Grandparents in Australia can legally support their grandchildren in saving for their first home while simultaneously enhancing their own age pension. The strategy relies on a combination of Centrelin...

item.title: Pakistani Armed Forces Conduct Targeted Strikes on Afghan Taliban Installations in Kabul and Nangarhar
item.description: On the night of March 16, 2026, Pakistani forces carried out precision airstrikes on military installations of the Afghan Taliban in Kabul and Nangarhar, as part of Operation Ghazab lil-Haq. Informati...

item.title: Mantashe Calls for Domestic Oil and Gas Development Amid Rising Global Fuel Prices
item.description: Mineral and Petroleum Resources Minister Gwede Mantashe has urged South Africa to fast-track its oil and gas sector to safeguard against global energy volatility. Speaking at the Southern Africa Oil a...

item.title: Study Suggests Ancient Greenland Cooling Event Weakened Indian Monsoon
item.description: A recent study highlights that approximately 8,200 years ago, a significant cooling event occurred in Greenland, which had far-reaching impacts on global climate patterns. This research indicates that...

item.title: Nvidia Launches NemoClaw to Provide Enterprise-Grade Security for AI Agents
item.description: During the 2026 GTC keynote, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang introduced NemoClaw, a new enterprise AI agent platform built on top of the open-source OpenClaw framework. NemoClaw enhances OpenClaw with advance...

item.title: Canadian Filmmakers Achieve Multiple Wins at the 2026 Academy Awards
item.description: Montreal-based filmmakers Chris Lavis and Maciek Szczerbowski have brought Canada into the spotlight at the 2026 Academy Awards by winning the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film with their stop-motion...

item.title: Tanzania, Mozambique and Malawi Unite to Protect Ruvuma River Basin with GEF Support
item.description: Tanzania, Mozambique na Malawi wameshirikiana kuanzisha mradi wa dola 7.12 million kupitia Global Environment Facility (GEF) kulinda mto Ruvuma Basin. Mto huu mkubwa unaunganisha milima ya kusini mwa ...

item.title: British Columbia recruits over 400 U.S. health-care professionals to address staffing needs
item.description: Between March 2025 and January 2026, British Columbia successfully recruited 414 health-care workers from the United States, including 89 doctors, 42 nurse practitioners, 260 nurses, and 23 allied hea...

item.title: Former DPWH Official Testifies About P130-M Kickbacks from Zaldy Co
item.description: Former DPWH Undersecretary Roberto Bernardo testified before the Sandiganbayan Sixth Division that former Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co gave him a total of P130 million as a 'balato,' or token of...

item.title: Trump criticises UK stance on Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has expressed disappointment with the United Kingdom over its response to the escalating Iran conflict, particularly regarding efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a criti...
